Explanation: The clues point to Edward Teller, who received his doctorate from the University of Leipzig (founded in the 15th century, but he also studied at the University of Munich, founded in the 15th century, and the University of Karlsruhe, founded in the 19th century). He shares his surname with André Teller, a Senegalese politician. Edward Teller received advice from J. Robert Oppenheimer, known as the "father of the atomic bomb." Several scientific concepts are named after Edward Teller, including the "Gamow–Teller transition," "Jahn–Teller effect," and "Brunauer–Emmett–Teller theory" [98371]. The most widely recognized is the "Jahn–Teller effect."
Exact Answer: Jahn–Teller effect
Confidence: 90%
